name: Mritunjay Pratap Singh
title: Backend Software Engineer
tagline: Building scalable microservices, event-driven systems & REST APIs
summary: Backend Software Engineer specializing in high-throughput distributed systems, event-driven microservices, and REST APIs. Delivered 600x performance improvement in production payment pipeline through async architecture, batch prefetch optimization, and concurrent processing. Building systems processing 100K+ daily transactions with Python, FastAPI, and AWS.

highlights: Achieved 600x throughput improvement in payment processing engine by implementing batch prefetch (19,000 → 10 DB queries/chunk), asyncio.gather() concurrent processing, and race-safe multi-pod architecture, enabling 100K+ records in 54 min (previously timed out at 1K).

Architected multi-channel Communication Aggregator processing 100K+ daily messages across WhatsApp/SMS/RCS with event-driven AWS SQS pipelines, multi-database integration (MongoDB, MySQL, MSSQL, OpenSearch), and 174+ automated tests.

Designed multi-vendor Dialer Service with Weighted Round Robin routing, circuit breaker for automatic vendor failover, and RBI-compliant number selection using Redis-backed state management, reducing manual effort by 80% for 50+ agents.

Built 6-stage async payment pipeline (Upload → Validation → Realization → Bank Response → Reversal → Bounce Recovery) with SQS FIFO queues, dual-database async I/O (PostgreSQL + MSSQL), and semaphore-controlled parallel API calls achieving 25x speedup.

Engineered Collection Allocation Engine with Haversine-based proximity matching (lat-long → pincode → branch → cross-cluster), weight-based distribution, and executive stickiness algorithm for fair workload across 4 product tracks.
Developed internal automation tools using Django, DRF, and PostgreSQL, reducing student query resolution time by 40%.
Built and optimized REST APIs for real-time code evaluation serving 500+ concurrent students.

projects.name: Receivable Engine — NACH/Cheque Processing
projects.description: High-throughput payment reconciliation system with 6-stage event-driven pipeline processing 100K+ records.
highlights: Achieved 600x per-record speedup (20s → 0.03s) via batch prefetch eliminating N+1 queries, reducing 19,000 DB calls to 10 per validation chunk.
Implemented race-safe multi-pod processing with atomic status transitions (UPDATE WHERE status=X), enabling horizontal scaling with zero duplicate processing.
Parallelized external API calls using asyncio.Semaphore(25) with gather(), achieving 25x throughput improvement for realization and reversal workflows.
Designed worker-consumer architecture with graceful shutdown, isolated DB sessions per message, and mini-transactions to minimize lock contention.

projects.name: Communication Aggregator Service
projects.description: Multi-tenant communication platform processing 100K+ daily messages across WhatsApp, SMS, and RCS for 4 business verticals.
highlights: Designed event-driven architecture with SQS async batch processing, Redis + RQ job scheduling, and real-time webhook consumers for delivery status tracking.
Built extensible vertical system using Registry pattern (CRM, LMS, LOS, MSME) with polymorphic routing for campaign management and lead processing.
Implemented exponential backoff retry mechanism with persistent retry queue, reducing failed message rate and improving delivery reliability.
Integrated 5 databases (MongoDB, MySQL, MSSQL, Redshift, OpenSearch) with connection pooling and health checks; achieved 174+ test coverage.

projects.name: Multi-Vendor Dialer Service
projects.description: Telephony orchestration platform with intelligent traffic routing across Ozonetel, Knowlarity, and Exotel.
highlights: Implemented Weighted Round Robin router with Redis-backed cursor management and TTL-based daily reset for campaign-isolated traffic distribution.
Built circuit breaker pattern tracking consecutive failures per vendor with automatic degradation, half-open recovery, and configurable thresholds.
Designed RBI-compliant number selector with series-based WRR, customer preference tracking, and failure-based series blocking using Redis state machine.
Applied Factory + Strategy + Template Method patterns for vendor abstraction — adding new vendors requires zero changes to existing code.

projects.name: Collection Allocation Engine
projects.description: Geography-aware, weight-based case allocation engine replacing manual distribution across 4 product tracks.
highlights: Designed pure-function allocation core (no I/O, no side effects) with 4-level proximity ladder: Haversine lat-long → pincode → branch → cross-cluster matching.
Built dual allocation models: weight-based for soft bucket and case-count/POS for hard bucket with executive stickiness for continuity.
Implemented async MSSQL with aioodbc, dual read/write connection pools, and geocode caching to minimize external API calls.
YAML-driven team configuration for weight grids and capacity rules, enabling business teams to modify allocation without code changes.

education.institution: Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam Technical University
